author: will_allred
title: Email Length and Reply Rates
content:
The data is clear: short emails win.

We analyzed 10 million cold emails and here's what we found:
- Emails under 50 words get a 17% reply rate.
- Emails with 50-100 words get a 12% reply rate.
- Emails over 100 words drop to under 7% reply rate.

Why?
Because people read their emails on mobile devices while doing something else.
If they see a wall of text, they archive it.

Keep it simple:
1. Hook: Mention a specific trigger (news, hire, tool).
2. Problem: Poke a known pain point.
3. Value: One line of how you solve it.
4. Call to Action: A simple low-friction question (e.g. 'Are you open to looking at the data?').

Stop pitching. Start conversation.

author: will_allred
title: The No-Friction CTA
content:
Most cold email CTAs fail because they ask for too much, too soon.

'Are you free for 30 minutes on Thursday at 2 PM?'

This is a huge ask for a stranger. You are asking them to commit time they don't have.

Instead, use a low-friction CTA:
- 'Open to checking it out?'
- 'Would you want to see the template we used?'
- 'Is this on your radar for Q3?'

Lower the bar. Get the reply first. You can schedule the meeting later.

author: josh_braun
title: Poke the Bear Framework
content:
Traditional pitching: 'Hi, we are the leading provider of X and we help companies do Y...'

Result: Delete.

Instead of pitching, 'poke the bear'. State a common, painful problem in a neutral tone and ask if they are experiencing it.

Example:
'Most VP of Sales I talk to tell me their reps are spending 3 hours a day manually researching leads instead of calling them.

How are you making sure your reps stay focused on selling?'

This starts a dialogue, not a sales pitch.

author: josh_braun
title: Radically Honest Cold Calls
content:
When someone answers a cold call, they are immediately on the defensive. They know it's a sales pitch.

Defuse the tension by being radically honest.

'Hi Sarah, this is Josh. We've never spoken before, and to be completely transparent, this is a cold call. I know you're busy. Would you like to hang up now, or give me 30 seconds to explain why I called, and then decide?'

9 out of 10 times, they laugh and say 'Go ahead.'

Permission-based selling works.

author: jed_mahrle
title: The 3-Step Follow-Up Sequence
content:
Stop sending 'just bumping this' follow-ups. They add zero value.

Here is a 3-step follow-up sequence that gets replies:

Day 1: The Core Pitch (Focus on problem + trigger).
Day 3: The Asset Drop ('Saw you publish X, thought this guide on Y might be useful. No pitch.').
Day 7: The Direct Question ('Are you still focusing on X this quarter, or should I stop reaching out?').

Every touchpoint should either teach them something new or make it extremely easy to say 'No'.

author: eric_nowoslawski
title: Domain Setup Checklist
content:
If you are sending cold emails from your primary company domain, you are playing Russian roulette with your brand.

Always buy secondary domains. Here's my checklist:
1. Buy domains on Porkbun or Cloudflare (e.g. if company is 'getxyz.com', buy 'tryxyz.com' or 'usexyz.com').
2. Set up Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 (max 2-3 inboxes per domain).
3. Configure DNS records: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC (p=quarantine or p=none to start).
4. Warm up inboxes for 14-21 days using smart tools like Smartlead.
5. Keep daily sending limit to 30-40 emails per inbox maximum.

author: nick_abraham
title: Cold Email Scale Playbook
content:
How we send 50,000 cold emails a month for B2B SaaS clients:

- 50 secondary domains.
- 100 total inboxes (2 per domain).
- Smartlead for master inbox rotation and sequence orchestration.
- Clay for list enrichment and custom AI-written opening lines.
- Double verification: Run lists through NeverBounce + Scrubby (to catch catch-all emails).

If you don't build this level of infrastructure, you'll land in spam by week 3.

author: guillaume_moubeche
title: Using Personalized Images
content:
People buy from people, not logos.

Instead of sending plain text, we use lemlist's dynamic image generation to show a photo of the sales rep holding a coffee cup with the prospect's company logo on it.

Reply rates went from 8% to 22%.

Personalization isn't just about putting their name in the subject line. It's about showing that you actually took 5 minutes to think about them.

author: jesse_ouellette
title: Why Warmup Pools Aren't Enough
content:
Many people think they can buy a domain, throw it in a warmup pool for 2 weeks, and send 500 emails a day.

That era is over. ESPs (Google & Microsoft) are tracking sender reputation based on domain age, traffic patterns, and spam reports.

Warmup pools simulate positive engagement, but if your actual campaigns have a high bounce rate or low reply rate, your domain reputation will plummet instantly.

Focus on data quality. A clean list is the best deliverability tool.

author: florian_decludt
title: Cold Email Teardown: Before and After
content:
BEFORE:
'Dear Sir/Madam, we are a leading software agency offering state of the art design and development. We would love to hop on a call to see how we can assist you...'

AFTER:
'Hi John, noticed your company just hired 3 new engineers. Are you still spending hours onboarding them onto your legacy codebase, or did you automate that workflow?'

Keep it focused on their situation, not your offer.

author: justin_michael
title: The Neuro-Map Sequence Method
content:
To break through to enterprise C-suite buyers, you need a high-frequency, multi-channel approach.

My Neuro-Map JMM method:
- Day 1: LinkedIn connection request + view profile.
- Day 1 (10 mins later): Cold email (short, mobile-optimized).
- Day 2: Cold call + VM (no pitch, just direct problem reference).
- Day 4: LinkedIn message.
- Day 7: Direct email with a quick video walk-through.

Be persistent. It takes 12-15 touches to get through to a busy executive.

author: alex_berman
title: The Golden Cold Email Script
content:
This one script has generated over $1 million in sales:

Subject: Quick question for {Company}

Hi {First Name},

I was looking at your website and noticed you don't have any video testimonials from your clients. We built a tool that helps SaaS companies collect and embed video reviews in under 5 minutes.

We recently helped client X increase their conversions by 20% using this.

Would you be open to seeing a quick 30-second demo?

Thanks,
Alex
